title: Flaveria bidentis
intro: Flaveria bidentis, also known as yellow tops, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, commonly found in the southern United States and Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, potentially providing health benefits such as wound healing and immune system support. The bioactive constituents of Flaveria bidentis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Flaveria bidentis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is commonly used to make teas, tinctures, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems.
taxonomy: Flaveria bidentis is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it among the flowering plants, specifically within the clade of core eudicots. The genus Flaveria is further grouped under the tribe Eupatorieae, which is a subclade of the asterid clade. As part of the asterid clade, Flaveria bidentis shares characteristics with other flowering plants that have a distinct morphology and reproductive strategy. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared traits with other members of the plant kingdom.
